continuing after an error\n    let results: Vec<Result<String>> = vec![\n        Err(ParseError::syntax(\"error 1\").with_line(1)),\n        Err(ParseError::validation(\"error 2\").with_line(2)),\n        Err(ParseError::io(\"error 3\").with_line(3)),\n    ];\n\n    let errors: Vec<ParseError> = results\n        .into_iter()\n        .filter_map(|r| r.err())\n        .collect();\n\n    assert_eq!(errors.len(), 3);\n    assert!(errors[0].is_syntax());\n    assert!(errors[1].is_validation());\n    assert!(errors[2].is_io());\n\n    // All errors have their line numbers\n    assert_eq!(errors[0].line, Some(1));\n    assert_eq!(errors[1].line, Some(2));\n    assert_eq!(errors[2].line, Some(3));\n}\n","numLines":617,"startLine":1,"totalLines":617}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_d6b3512a67c447d9ac75141d","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Error types for YAML parsing\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module defines the error types used throughout the YAML parser.\n4\t//!\n5\t//! # Error Handling Philosophy\n6\t//!\n7\t//! The `ParseError` type follows a structured approach to error handling that emphasizes:\n8\t//!\n9\t//! 1. **Clear categorization** - Each error falls into a distinct category (syntax, I/O, validation, etc.)\n10\t//! 2. **Rich context** - Errors carry location information, context messages, and code snippets\n11\t//! 3. **Composability** - Errors propagate cleanly through the call stack using Rust's `?` operator\n12\t//! 4. **User-friendly output** - Multiple formatting options for different use cases (logging, UI, debugging)\n13\t//!\n14\t//! ## When to Use Each Variant\n15\t//!\n16\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::Io` vs Other Variants\n17\t//!\n18\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Io`] for **file system and I/O operation failures**:\n19\t//! - File not found (`std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound`)\n20\t//! - Permission denied (`std::io::ErrorKind::PermissionDenied`)\n21\t//! - Read/write failures (`std::io::ErrorKind::Other`)\n22\t//! - Network I/O errors (if applicable)\n23\t//!\n24\t//! **Do not use** `Io` for:\n25\t//! - YAML syntax errors → use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n26\t//! - Type mismatches → use [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`]\n27\t//! - Constraint violations → use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`]\n28\t//!\n29\t//! ```ignore\n30\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use Io for file system errors\n31\t//! let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;  // io::Error → Io\n32\t//!\n33\t//! // ❌ Incorrect: Don't use Io for parsing errors\n34\t//! // let error = ParseError::io(\"invalid YAML structure\");  // Wrong! Use Syntax instead\n35\t//! ```\n36\t//!\n37\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8` vs Other Variants\n38\t//!\n39\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`] specifically for **encoding errors**:\n40\t//! - Invalid UTF-8 byte sequences in input\n41\t//! - Failed UTF-8 validation when converting bytes to strings\n42\t//!\n43\t//! **Do not use** `InvalidUtf8` for:\n44\t//! - I/O errors during file reading → use [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n45\t//! - General parsing failures → use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n46\t//!\n47\t//! ```ignore\n48\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use InvalidUtf8 for encoding errors\n49\t//! let string = String::from_utf8(bytes)?;  // FromUtf8Error → InvalidUtf8\n50\t//!\n51\t//! // ❌ Incorrect: Don't use InvalidUtf8 for file reading errors\n52\t//! // let error = ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8);  // Wrong! Use Io instead\n53\t//! ```\n54\t//!\n55\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof` vs `ParseErrorKind::Syntax`\n56\t//!\n57\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof`] when **input ends prematurely**:\n58\t//! - Incomplete YAML documents (missing closing brackets, braces, quotes)\n59\t//! - Truncated files or streams\n60\t//! - Multi-document YAML streams that end mid-document\n61\t//!\n62\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`] for **general YAML syntax violations**:\n63\t//! - Invalid indentation\n64\t//! - Invalid escape sequences\n65\t//! - Malformed scalars or mappings\n66\t//! - Any YAML grammar violation that's not specifically EOF-related\n67\t//!\n68\t//! ```ignore\n69\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use UnexpectedEof for incomplete input\n70\t//! if input.ends_with(\"key: \") {\n71\t//!     return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof));\n72\t//! }\n73\t//!\n74\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use Syntax for general YAML errors\n75\t//! if !is_valid_indentation(line) {\n76\t//!     return Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid indentation\"));\n77\t//! }\n78\t//! ```\n79\t//!\n80\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch` vs `ParseErrorKind::Validation`\n81\t//!\n82\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`] when **a value has the wrong Rust/YAML type**:\n83\t//! - Expecting an integer but finding a string\n84\t//! - Expecting a sequence but finding a scalar\n85\t//! - Expecting a boolean but finding a number\n86\t//!\n87\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`] for **semantic constraint violations**:\n88\t//! - Value out of allowed range (e.g., port number > 65535)\n89\t//! - String doesn't match required pattern (e.g., invalid email format)\n90\t//! - Array length constraints violated\n91\t//! - Business logic or schema validation failures\n92\t//!\n93\t//! The key distinction: `TypeMismatch` is about **type**, `Validation` is about **value**.\n94\t//!\n95\t//! ```ignore\n96\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use TypeMismatch for type errors\n97\t//! let port = value[\"port\"].as_i64()\n98\t//!     .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\"))?;\n99\t//!\n100\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use Validation for value constraints\n101\t//! if port < 1 || port > 65535 {\n102\t//!     return Err(ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\"));\n103\t//! }\n104\t//! ```\n105\t//!\n106\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey` vs `ParseErrorKind::Validation`\n107\t//!\n108\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey`] specifically for **duplicate key errors**:\n109\t//! - YAML mappings with repeated keys (YAML 1.2 spec violation)\n110\t//! - Configuration files with conflicting entries\n111\t//!\n112\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`] for **general validation failures** including:\n113\t//! - Missing required fields (not duplicated, but absent)\n114\t//! - Inter-field constraint violations (e.g., \"start_time > end_time\")\n115\t//! - Schema validation failures beyond type/duplicate checking\n116\t//!\n117\t//! ```ignore\n118\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use DuplicateKey for duplicate keys\n119\t//! if keys.contains(&key) {\n120\t//!     return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey(key)));\n121\t//! }\n122\t//!\n123\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use Validation for missing required fields\n124\t//! if value[\"name\"].is_null() {\n125\t//!     return Err(ParseError::validation(\"missing required field: 'name'\"));\n126\t//! }\n127\t//! ```\n128\t//!\n129\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::Other` (Catch-all) vs Specific Variants\n130\t//!\n131\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Other`] **only as a last resort** for errors that don't fit other categories:\n132\t//! - Unclassifiable serde_yaml errors\n133\t//! - Errors from external libraries that don't map cleanly\n134\t//! - Temporary error cases during refactoring\n135\t//!\n136\t//! **Prefer specific variants** whenever possible:\n137\t//! - Unknown anchors → [`ParseErrorKind::UnknownAnchor`]\n138\t//! - Syntax errors → [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n139\t//! - I/O errors → [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n140\t//! - Type errors → [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`]\n141\t//!\n142\t//! If you find yourself using `Other` frequently, consider adding a new specific variant.\n143\t//!\n144\t//! ```ignore\n145\t//! // ✅ Prefer specific variants\n146\t//! return Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\"));\n147\t//!\n148\t//! // ⚠️ Use Other only as a catch-all\n149\t//! return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::Other(\"unclassified error\".to_string())));\n150\t//! ```\n151\t//!\n152\t//! # Error Propagation Strategy\n153\t//!\n154\t//! The `ParseError` type is designed to work seamlessly with Rust's error propagation\n155\t//! mechanisms. It implements `From` for common error types, enabling the use of the `?`\n156\t//! operator for automatic error conversion.\n157\t//!\n158\t//! ## Basic Propagation with `?`\n159\t//!\n160\t//! ```ignore\n161\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, Result};\n162\t//!\n163\t//! fn parse_config(path: &Path) -> Result<Config> {\n164\t//!     let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;  // io::Error → ParseError\n165\t//!     parse_yaml(&content)\n166\t//! }\n167\t//! ```\n168\t//!\n169\t//! ## Adding Context with `.context()`\n170\t//!\n171\t//! Use the builder-style `.context()` method to add contextual information:\n172\t//!\n173\t//! ```ignore\n174\t//! fn parse_database_config(value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<DatabaseConfig> {\n175\t//!     let port = value[\"port\"]\n176\t//!         .as_i64()\n177\t//!         .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"null\"))\n178\t//!         .context(\"while parsing database configuration\")?;\n179\t//!\n180\t//!     Ok(DatabaseConfig { port })\n181\t//! }\n182\t//! ```\n183\t//!\n184\t//! ## Converting from Other Error Types\n185\t//!\n186\t//! `ParseError` implements `From` for:\n187\t//! - `std::io::Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n188\t//! - `serde_yaml::Error` → Appropriate [`ParseErrorKind`] based on error classification\n189\t//! - `std::str::Utf8Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`]\n190\t//! - `std::string::FromUtf8Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`]\n191\t//!\n192\t//! ## Custom Error Creation\n193\t//!\n194\t//! For domain-specific errors, create them directly:\n195\t//!\n196\t//! ```ignore\n197\t//! fn validate_port(port: i64) -> Result<u16> {\n198\t//!     if port < 1 || port > 65535 {\n199\t//!         return Err(ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\"));\n200\t//!     }\n201\t//!     Ok(port as u16)\n202\t//! }\n203\t//! ```\n204\t//!\n205\t//! # Examples\n206\t//!\n207\t//! ## Basic Error Creation\n208\t//!\n209\t//! Creating errors using convenience constructors:\n210\t//!\n211\t//! ```\n212\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n213\t//!\n214\t//! // Create a syntax error\n215\t//! let syntax_err = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML indentation\");\n216\t//! assert!(syntax_err.is_syntax());\n217\t//!\n218\t//! // Create a validation error\n219\t//! let validation_err = ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\");\n220\t//! assert!(validation_err.is_validation());\n221\t//!\n222\t//! // Create a type mismatch error\n223\t//! let type_err = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\");\n224\t//! assert!(type_err.is_type_mismatch());\n225\t//! assert_eq!(type_err.summary(), \"<unknown>: type mismatch at 'port': expected integer, got string\");\n226\t//! ```\n227\t//!\n228\t//! ## Error Propagation with `?`\n229\t//!\n230\t//! Using the `?` operator to automatically convert errors:\n231\t//!\n232\t//! ```\n233\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, Result};\n234\t//! use std::fs;\n235\t//!\n236\t//! fn read_config(path: &str) -> Result<String> {\n237\t//!     // io::Error is automatically converted to ParseError via From impl\n238\t//!     let content = fs::read_to_string(path)?;\n239\t//!     Ok(content)\n240\t//! }\n241\t//!\n242\t//! // This function demonstrates automatic error conversion\n243\t//! fn parse_config_size(path: &str) -> Result<usize> {\n244\t//!     let content = read_config(path)?;  // ParseError propagates via ?\n245\t//!     Ok(content.len())\n246\t//! }\n247\t//!\n248\t//! // Example: successful read\n249\t//! assert!(parse_config_size(\"/dev/null\").is_ok());\n250\t//! ```\n251\t//!\n252\t//! ## Custom Error Handling with Builder Pattern\n253\t//!\n254\t//! Using builder methods to add context and location information:\n255\t//!\n256\t//! ```\n257\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n258\t//!\n259\t//! fn validate_service_config(yaml_content: &str) -> Result<(), ParseError> {\n260\t//!     // Simulating an error during validation\n261\t//!     if yaml_content.contains(\"port: abc\") {\n262\t//!         let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"service.port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n263\t//!             .with_path(\"config/services.yaml\")\n264\t//!             .with_line(5)\n265\t//!             .with_column(10)\n266\t//!             .with_context(\"while validating service configuration\")\n267\t//!             .with_snippet(\"services:\\n  - name: web\\n    port: abc\");\n268\t//!\n269\t//!         return Err(error);\n270\t//!     }\n271\t//!     Ok(())\n272\t//! }\n273\t//!\n274\t//! // Create an error to verify the builder pattern\n275\t//! let error = ParseError::validation(\"invalid port\")\n276\t//!     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n277\t//!     .with_line(10)\n278\t//!     .with_column(5)\n279\t//!     .with_context(\"while parsing configuration\");\n280\t//!\n281\t//! assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n282\t//! assert_eq!(error.line, Some(10));\n283\t//! assert_eq!(error.column, Some(5));\n284\t//! assert_eq!(error.context, \"while parsing configuration\");\n285\t//! ```\n286\t//!\n287\t//! ## Error Display and Formatting\n288\t//!\n289\t//! Different ways to format and display errors:\n290\t//!\n291\t//! ```\n292\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n293\t//!\n294\t//! // Create an error with full context\n295\t//! let error = ParseError::syntax(\"unexpected colon\")\n296\t//!     .with_path(\"config/database.yaml\")\n297\t//!     .with_line(15)\n298\t//!     .with_column(8)\n299\t//!     .with_context(\"while parsing database configuration\")\n300\t//!     .with_snippet(\"database:\\n  host: localhost\\n  port: 5432:\\n    invalid\");\n301\t//!\n302\t//! // Get a single-line summary (good for logging)\n303\t//! let summary = error.summary();\n304\t//! assert!(summary.contains(\"config/database.yaml:15\"));\n305\t//! assert!(summary.contains(\"syntax error\"));\n306\t//! assert!(summary.contains(\"while parsing database configuration\"));\n307\t//!\n308\t//! // Get a detailed multi-line report (good for user display)\n309\t//! let detailed = error.detailed_report();\n310\t//! assert!(detailed.contains(\"error:\"));\n311\t//! assert!(detailed.contains(\"snippet:\"));\n312\t//!\n313\t//! // Get a structured representation (good for debugging)\n314\t//! let structured = error.format_structured();\n315\t//! assert!(structured.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n316\t//! assert!(structured.contains(\"config/database.yaml:15:8\"));\n317\t//! assert!(structured.contains(\"line: Some(15)\"));\n318\t//!\n319\t//! // Display implementation provides user-friendly output\n320\t//! let display_string = format!(\"{}\", error);\n321\t//! assert!(display_string.contains(\"config/database.yaml\"));\n322\t//! assert!(display_string.contains(\"syntax error\"));\n323\t//! ```\n324\t//!\n325\t//! ## Error Conversion from Standard Types\n326\t//!\n327\t//! Automatic conversion from standard library errors:\n328\t//!\n329\t//! ```\n330\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n331\t//! use std::io;\n332\t//!\n333\t//! // io::Error converts to ParseError::Io\n334\t//! let io_err = io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::NotFound, \"file not found\");\n335\t//! let parse_err: ParseError = io_err.into();\n336\t//! assert!(parse_err.is_io());\n337\t//! assert!(parse_err.summary().contains(\"I/O error\"));\n338\t//! assert!(parse_err.summary().contains(\"file not found\"));\n339\t//!\n340\t//! // String::from_utf8 Error converts to ParseError::InvalidUtf8\n341\t//! let invalid_bytes = b\"\\xff\\xfe\\xfd\";\n342\t//! let utf8_err = String::from_utf8(invalid_bytes.to_vec()).unwrap_err();\n343\t//! let parse_err: ParseError = utf8_err.into();\n344\t//! assert!(matches!(parse_err.kind, armor::parsers::yaml::ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8));\n345\t//! ```\n346\t//!\n347\t//! ## Working with Error Types\n348\t//!\n349\t//! Checking error types and handling specific cases:\n350\t//!\n351\t//! ```\n352\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n353\t//!\n354\t//! let errors = vec![\n355\t//!     ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\"),\n356\t//!     ParseError::io(\"file not found\"),\n357\t//!     ParseError::validation(\"port out of range\"),\n358\t//!     ParseError::type_mismatch(\"field\", \"string\", \"integer\"),\n359\t//! ];\n360\t//!\n361\t//! // Check specific error types\n362\t//! assert!(errors[0].is_syntax());\n363\t//! assert!(errors[1].is_io());\n364\t//! assert!(errors[2].is_validation());\n365\t//! assert!(errors[3].is_type_mismatch());\n366\t//!\n367\t//! // Filter errors by type\n368\t//! let syntax_errors: Vec<_> = errors.iter()\n369\t//!     .filter(|e| e.is_syntax())\n370\t//!     .collect();\n371\t//! assert_eq!(syntax_errors.len(), 1);\n372\t//! ```\n373\t\n374\tuse std::fmt;\n375\t\n376\t/// Main error type for YAML parsing operations\n377\t#[derive(Clone)]\n378\tpub struct ParseError {\n379\t    /// The kind of error that occurred\n380\t    pub kind: ParseErrorKind,\n381\t    /// The line number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n382\t    pub line: Option<usize>,\n383\t    /// The column number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n384\t    pub column: Option<usize>,\n385\t    /// The file or source path where the error occurred\n386\t    pub path: Option<String>,\n387\t    /// A code snippet showing the problematic segment\n388\t    pub snippet: Option<String>,\n389\t    /// Additional context about the error\n390\t    pub context: String,\n391\t}\n392\t\n393\timpl ParseError {\n394\t    /// Create a new ParseError with the given kind\n395\t    ///\n396\t    /// This is the base constructor for creating a ParseError. All fields except\n397\t    /// `kind` are set to their default values (None or empty). Use the builder methods\n398\t    /// to add additional context and location information.\n399\t    ///\n400\t    /// # Examples\n401\t    ///\n402\t    /// ```\n403\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, ParseErrorKind};\n404\t    ///\n405\t    /// let error = ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof);\n406\t    /// assert!(matches!(error.kind, ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof));\n407\t    /// assert_eq!(error.line, None);\n408\t    /// ```\n409\t    ///\n410\t    /// For common error types, prefer using the convenience constructors:\n411\t    /// - [`ParseError::syntax()`] for syntax errors\n412\t    /// - [`ParseError::io()`] for I/O errors\n413\t    /// - [`ParseError::validation()`] for validation errors\n414\t    /// - [`ParseError::type_mismatch()`] for type mismatch errors\n415\t    pub fn new(kind: ParseErrorKind) -> Self {\n416\t        Self {\n417\t            kind,\n418\t            line: None,\n419\t            column: None,\n420\t            path: None,\n421\t            snippet: None,\n422\t            context: String::new(),\n423\t        }\n424\t    }\n425\t\n426\t    /// Set the line number for this error\n427\t    ///\n428\t    /// Line numbers are 1-indexed, matching typical text editor conventions.\n429\t    ///\n430\t    /// # Examples\n431\t    ///\n432\t    /// ```\n433\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n434\t    ///\n435\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n436\t    ///     .with_line(42);\n437\t    /// assert_eq!(error.line, Some(42));\n438\t    /// ```\n439\t    pub fn with_line(mut self, line: usize) -> Self {\n440\t        self.line = Some(line);\n441\t        self\n442\t    }\n443\t\n444\t    /// Set the column number for this error\n445\t    ///\n446\t    /// Column numbers are 1-indexed, indicating the character position within the line.\n447\t    ///\n448\t    /// # Examples\n449\t    ///\n450\t    /// ```\n451\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n452\t    ///\n453\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n454\t    ///     .with_column(15);\n455\t    /// assert_eq!(error.column, Some(15));\n456\t    /// ```\n457\t    pub fn with_column(mut self, column: usize) -> Self {\n458\t        self.column = Some(column);\n459\t        self\n460\t    }\n461\t\n462\t    /// Set the file/source path for this error\n463\t    ///\n464\t    /// The path can be absolute or relative. It's displayed in error messages to help\n465\t    /// users locate the source of the error.\n466\t    ///\n467\t    /// # Examples\n468\t    ///\n469\t    /// ```\n470\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n471\t    ///\n472\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\")\n473\t    ///     .with_path(\"config/services.yaml\");\n474\t    /// assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config/services.yaml\".to_string()));\n475\t    /// ```\n476\t    pub fn with_path(mut self, path: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n477\t        self.path = Some(path.into());\n478\t        self\n479\t    }\n480\t\n481\t    /// Set the code snippet for this error\n482\t    ///\n483\t    /// The snippet should contain the relevant lines of source code where the error occurred.\n484\t    /// This is displayed in detailed error reports to help users understand the context.\n485\t    ///\n486\t    /// # Examples\n487\t    ///\n488\t    /// ```\n489\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n490\t    ///\n491\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid port value\")\n492\t    ///     .with_line(5)\n493\t    ///     .with_snippet(\"service:\\n  name: web\\n  port: abc\");\n494\t    ///\n495\t    /// let report = error.detailed_report();\n496\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"service:\"));\n497\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"port: abc\"));\n498\t    /// ```\n499\t    pub fn with_snippet(mut self, snippet: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n500\t        self.snippet = Some(snippet.into());\n501\t        self\n502\t    }\n503\t\n504\t    /// Set the context message for this error\n505\t    ///\n506\t    /// Context provides additional information about what operation was being performed\n507\t    /// when the error occurred. This helps users understand the broader scenario.\n508\t    ///\n509\t    /// # Examples\n510\t    ///\n511\t    /// ```\n512\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n513\t    ///\n514\t    /// let error = ParseError::validation(\"port out of range\")\n515\t    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing database configuration\");\n516\t    ///\n517\t    /// let summary = error.summary();\n518\t    /// assert!(summary.contains(\"while parsing database configuration\"));\n519\t    /// ```\n520\t    pub fn with_context(mut self, context: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n521\t        self.context = context.into();\n522\t        self\n523\t    }\n524\t\n525\t    /// Set both line and column for this error\n526\t    ///\n527\t    /// Convenience method for setting location with a single call.\n528\t    ///\n529\t    /// # Examples\n530\t    ///\n531\t    /// ```\n532\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n533\t    ///\n534\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n535\t    ///     .with_location(10, 25);\n536\t    /// assert_eq!(error.line, Some(10));\n537\t    /// assert_eq!(error.column, Some(25));\n538\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"10:25\");\n539\t    /// ```\n540\t    pub fn with_location(mut self, line: usize, column: usize) -> Self {\n541\t        self.line = Some(line);\n542\t        self.column = Some(column);\n543\t        self\n544\t    }\n545\t\n546\t    /// Get a formatted location string (e.g., \"file.yaml:10:5\" or \"<unknown>:10\")\n547\t    ///\n548\t    /// This method generates a human-readable location string based on the available\n549\t    /// location information (path, line, column). The format adapts based on which fields are set.\n550\t    ///\n551\t    /// # Examples\n552\t    ///\n553\t    /// ```\n554\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n555\t    ///\n556\t    /// // Full location with path, line, and column\n557\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n558\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n559\t    ///     .with_location(10, 5);\n560\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"config.yaml:10:5\");\n561\t    ///\n562\t    /// // Line and column only\n563\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n564\t    ///     .with_location(42, 15);\n565\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"42:15\");\n566\t    ///\n567\t    /// // Path only\n568\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n569\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\");\n570\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"config.yaml\");\n571\t    ///\n572\t    /// // Unknown location\n573\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\");\n574\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"<unknown>\");\n575\t    /// ```\n576\t    pub fn location_string(&self) -> String {\n577\t        match (&self.path, self.line, self.column) {\n578\t            (Some(path), Some(line), Some(col)) => format!(\"{}:{}:{}\", path, line, col),\n579\t            (Some(path), Some(line), None) => format!(\"{}:{}\", path, line),\n580\t            (Some(path), None, Some(col)) => format!(\"{}::{}\", path, col),\n581\t            (Some(path), None, None) => path.clone(),\n582\t            (None, Some(line), Some(col)) => format!(\"{}:{}\", line, col),\n583\t            (None, Some(line), None) => format!(\"{}\", line),\n584\t            (None, None, Some(col)) => format!(\"col {}\", col),\n585\t            (None, None, None) => \"<unknown>\".to_string(),\n586\t        }\n587\t    }\n588\t\n589\t    /// Get a brief summary of the error (single line, suitable for logging)\n590\t    ///\n591\t    /// The summary includes location, error kind, and context (if set). This is ideal\n592\t    /// for logging purposes where a compact, single-line error message is desired.\n593\t    ///\n594\t    /// # Examples\n595\t    ///\n596\t    /// ```\n597\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n598\t    ///\n599\t    /// // Summary with context\n600\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n601\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n602\t    ///     .with_line(10)\n603\t    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing services\");\n604\t    /// assert_eq!(error.summary(),\n605\t    ///     \"config.yaml:10: syntax error: invalid token - while parsing services\");\n606\t    ///\n607\t    /// // Summary without context\n608\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n609\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n610\t    ///     .with_line(10);\n611\t    /// assert_eq!(error.summary(),\n612\t    ///     \"config.yaml:10: syntax error: invalid token\");\n613\t    /// ```\n614\t    pub fn summary(&self) -> String {\n615\t        let location = self.location_string();\n616\t        if !self.context.is_empty() {\n617\t            format!(\"{}: {} - {}\", location, self.kind, self.context)\n618\t        } else {\n619\t            format!(\"{}: {}\", location, self.kind)\n620\t        }\n621\t    }\n622\t\n623\t    /// Get a detailed multi-line error report with snippet and visual indicator\n624\t    ///\n625\t    /// This method generates a comprehensive error report suitable for displaying\n626\t    /// to users. It includes the error summary, context (if set), and a code snippet\n627\t    /// with a visual indicator (^) pointing to the error location.\n628\t    ///\n629\t    /// # Examples\n630\t    ///\n631\t    /// ```\n632\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n633\t    ///\n634\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid port value\")\n635\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n636\t    ///     .with_line(5)\n637\t    ///     .with_column(10)\n638\t    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing service configuration\")\n639\t    ///     .with_snippet(\"service:\\n  port: abc\");\n640\t    ///\n641\t    /// let report = error.detailed_report();\n642\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"error:\"));\n643\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"config.yaml:5:10\"));\n644\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"syntax error: invalid port value\"));\n645\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"while parsing service configuration\"));\n646\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"snippet:\"));\n647\t    /// ```\n648\t    pub fn detailed_report(&self) -> String {\n649\t        let mut report = String::new();\n650\t\n651\t        // Header line with location and error kind\n652\t        report.push_str(&format!(\"error: {}\\n\", self.summary()));\n653\t\n654\t        // Add context section if present\n655\t        if !self.context.is_empty() {\n656\t            report.push_str(&format!(\"  context: {}\\n\", self.context));\n657\t        }\n658\t\n659\t        // Add snippet with visual indicator\n660\t        if let Some(snippet) = &self.snippet {\n661\t            report.push_str(\"\\n  snippet:\\n\");\n662\t            for line in snippet.lines() {\n663\t                report.push_str(&format!(\"    {}\\n\", line));\n664\t            }\n665\t\n666\t            // Add visual indicator for column position\n667\t            if let Some(col) = self.column {\n668\t                if col > 0 {\n669\t                    report.push_str(&format!(\"    {}^\\n\", \" \".repeat(col.saturating_sub(1))));\n670\t                }\n671\t            }\n672\t        }\n673\t\n674\t        report\n675\t    }\n676\t\n677\t    /// Format this error as a structured log entry (JSON-like but readable)\n678\t    ///\n679\t    /// This method produces a structured representation of the error suitable for\n680\t    /// programmatic analysis or structured logging systems.\n681\t    ///\n682\t    /// # Examples\n683\t    ///\n684\t    /// ```\n685\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n686\t    ///\n687\t    /// let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n688\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n689\t    ///     .with_line(8)\n690\t    ///     .with_column(10);\n691\t    ///\n692\t    /// let formatted = error.format_structured();\n693\t    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n694\t    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"config.yaml:8:10\"));\n695\t    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"line: Some(8)\"));\n696\t    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"column: Some(10)\"));\n697\t    /// ```\n698\t    pub fn format_structured(&self) -> String {\n699\t        format!(\n700\t            \"ParseError {{ kind: {:?}, location: {}, line: {:?}, column: {:?} }}\",\n701\t            self.kind,\n702\t            self.location_string(),\n703\t            self.line,\n704\t            self.column\n705\t        )\n706\t    }\n707\t\n708\t    /// Create a syntax error\n709\t    pub fn syntax(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n710\t        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Syntax(msg.into()))\n711\t    }\n712\t\n713\t    /// Create an I/O error\n714\t    pub fn io(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n715\t        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Io(msg.into()))\n716\t    }\n717\t\n718\t    /// Create a validation error\n719\t    pub fn validation(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n720\t        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Validation(msg.into()))\n721\t    }\n722\t\n723\t    /// Create a type mismatch error\n724\t    ///\n725\t    /// # Arguments\n726\t    /// * `field` - The field path where the error occurred\n727\t    /// * `expected` - The expected type description\n728\t    /// * `actual` - The actual type that was received\n729\t    pub fn type_mismatch(field: impl Into<String>, matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::Syntax(_))\n740\t    }\n741\t\n742\t    /// Check if this is an I/O error\n743\t    pub fn is_io(&self) -> bool {\n744\t        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::Validation(_))\n750\t    }\n751\t\n752\t    /// Check if this is a type mismatch error\n753\t    pub fn is_type_mismatch(&self) -> bool {\n754\t        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch { .. })\n755\t    }\n756\t}\n757\t\n758\timpl fmt::Display for ParseError {\n759\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n760\t        // Use the summary method for concise display\n761\t        write!(f, \"{}\", self.summary())?;\n762\t\n763\t        // Add detailed section with snippet if available\n764\t        if let Some(snippet) =  {}\", \" \".repeat(col.saturating_sub(1)))?;\n774\t                    write!(f, \"^\")?;\n775\t                }\n776\t            }\n777\t        }\n778\t\n779\t        Ok(())\n780\t    }\n781\t}\n782\t\n783\timpl std::error::Error for ParseError {}\n784\t\n785\timpl PartialEq for ParseError {\n786\t    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n787\t        // Only compare core error identification fields\n788\t        // Context and snippet are NOT included in equality\n789\t        self.kind == other.kind\n790\t            && self.line == other.line\n791\t            && self.column == other.column\n792\t            && self.path == other.path\n793\t    }\n794\t}\n795\t\n796\timpl fmt::Debug for ParseError {\n797\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> .field(\"line\", &self.line)\n802\t            .field(\"column\", &self.column)\n803\t            .field(\"path\", &self.path)\n804\t            .field(\"context\", &self.context)\n805\t            .field(\"has_snippet\", &self.snippet.is_some())\n806\t            .finish()\n807\t    }\n808\t}\n809\t\n810\t/// The kind of parse error that occurred\n811\t///\n812\t/// This enum represents the core categories of errors that can occur during\n813\t/// YAML parsing operations. Each variant represents a distinct class of error.\n814\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\n815\tpub enum ParseErrorKind {\n816\t    /// Syntax error in the YAML source\n817\t    ///\n818\t    /// This variant covers errors related to invalid YAML structure, malformed\n819\t    /// syntax, or violations of the YAML grammar rules.\n820\t    Syntax(String),\n821\t\n822\t    /// I/O error (file not found, permission denied, etc.)\n823\t    ///\n824\t    /// This variant covers errors related to file system operations such as\n825\t    /// reading, writing, or accessing files.\n826\t    Io(String),\n827\t\n828\t    /// Validation error (constraint violations)\n829\t    ///\n830\t    /// This variant covers errors related to constraint violations such as\n831\t    /// required field violations, range violations, or other schema validation failures.\n832\t    Validation(String),\n833\t\n834\t    /// Type mismatch error (unexpected type for a field)\n835\t    ///\n836\t    /// This variant covers errors where a value has an unexpected type, such as\n837\t    /// expecting a string but receiving a number, or expecting a sequence but receiving a scalar.\n838\t    TypeMismatch {\n839\t        /// The field path where the error occurred\n840\t        field: String,\n841\t        /// The expected type\n842\t        expected: String,\n843\t        /// The actual type that was received\n844\t        actual: String,\n845\t    },\n846\t\n847\t    /// Unexpected end of input\n848\t    ///\n849\t    /// This variant covers errors where the YAML source ends prematurely\n850\t    /// and cannot be fully parsed.\n851\t    UnexpectedEof,\n852\t\n853\t    /// Invalid UTF-8 encoding\n854\t    ///\n855\t    /// This variant covers errors where the input contains invalid UTF-8 sequences.\n856\t    InvalidUtf8,\n857\t\n858\t    /// Unknown anchor or alias\n859\t    ///\n860\t    /// This variant covers errors where an anchor or alias reference cannot be resolved.\n861\t    UnknownAnchor(String),\n862\t\n863\t    /// Duplicate key in mapping\n864\t    ///\n865\t    /// This variant covers errors where duplicate keys are found in a YAML mapping.\n866\t    DuplicateKey(String),\n867\t\n868\t    /// Other error\n869\t    ///\n870\t    /// This variant provides a catch-all for errors that don't fit into any of the\n871\t    /// other specific categories, allowing for extensibility.\n872\t    Other(String),\n873\t}\n874\t\n875\timpl fmt::Display for ParseErrorKind {\n876\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n877\t        match self {\n878\t            Self::Syntax(msg) => write!(f, \"syntax error: {}\", msg),\n879\t            Self::Io(msg) => write!(f, \"I/O error: {}\", msg),\n880\t            Self::Validation(msg) => write!(f, \"validation error: {}\", msg),\n881\t            Self::TypeMismatch { field, expected, actual } => {\n882\t                write!(f, \"type mismatch at '{}': expected {}, got {}\", field, expected, actual)\n883\t            }\n884\t            Self::UnexpectedEof => write!(f, \"unexpected end of input\"),\n885\t            Self::InvalidUtf8 => write!(f, \"invalid UTF-8 encoding\"),\n886\t            Self::UnknownAnchor(name) => write!(f, \"unknown anchor: {}\", name),\n887\t            Self::DuplicateKey(key) => write!(f, \"duplicate key: {}\", key),\n888\t            Self::Other(msg) => write!(f, \"error: {}\", msg),\n889\t        }\n890\t    }\n891\t}\n892\t\n893\t/// Result type alias for parse operations\n894\tpub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, ParseError>;\n895\t\n896\t// ============================================================================\n897\t// From implementations for error propagation\n898\t// ============================================================================\n899\t\n900\timpl From<std::io::Error> for ParseError {\n901\t    fn from(err: std::io::Error) -> Self {\n902\t        ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::Io(err.to_string()))\n903\t    }\n904\t}\n905\t\n906\timpl From<serde_yaml::Error> for ParseError {\n907\t    fn from(err: serde_yaml::Error) -> Self {\n908\t        // Classify serde_yaml errors into appropriate ParseError kinds\n909\t        // based on the error message content\n910\t        let err_msg = err.to_string().to_lowercase();\n911\t\n912\t        let kind = if err_msg.contains(\"syntax\") || err_msg.contains(\"unexpected\") || err_msg.contains(\"expected\") {\n913\t            ParseErrorKind::Syntax(err.to_string())\n914\t        } else if err_msg.contains(\"duplicate\") {\n915\t            ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey(err.to_string())\n916\t        } else if err_msg.contains(\"io\") || err_msg.contains(\"failed to read\") {\n917\t            ParseErrorKind::Io(err.to_string())\n918\t        } else {\n919\t            ParseErrorKind::Other(err.to_string())\n920\t        };\n921\t\n922\t        ParseError::new(kind)\n923\t    }\n924\t}\n925\t\n926\timpl From<std::str::Utf8Error> for ParseError {\n927\t    fn from(err: std::str::Utf8Error) -> Self {\n928\t        ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8).with_context(err.to_string())\n929\t    }\n930\t}\n931\t\n932\timpl From<std::string::FromUtf8Error> for ParseError {\n933\t    fn from(err: std::string::FromUtf8Error) -> Self {\n934\t        ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8).with_context(err.to_string())\n935\t    }\n936\t}\n937\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"914ee606-06d9-4511-85b9-e1606196ac5a","uuid":"3000d1ed-59cc-47f4-a177-5ea4ca4503fb","timestamp":"2026-07-11T14:56:23.083Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/error.rs","content":"//! Error types for YAML parsing\n//!\n//! This module defines the error types used throughout the YAML parser.\n//!\n//! # Error Handling Philosophy\n//!\n//! The `ParseError` type follows a structured approach to error handling that emphasizes:\n//!\n//! 1. **Clear categorization** - Each error falls into a distinct category (syntax, I/O, validation, etc.)\n//! 2. **Rich context** - Errors carry location information, context messages, and code snippets\n//! 3. **Composability** - Errors propagate cleanly through the call stack using Rust's `?` operator\n//! 4. **User-friendly output** - Multiple formatting options for different use cases (logging, UI, debugging)\n//!\n//! ## When to Use Each Variant\n//!\n//! ### `ParseErrorKind::Io` vs Other Variants\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Io`] for **file system and I/O operation failures**:\n//! - File not found (`std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound`)\n//! - Permission denied (`std::io::ErrorKind::PermissionDenied`)\n//! - Read/write failures (`std::io::ErrorKind::Other`)\n//! - Network I/O errors (if applicable)\n//!\n//! **Do not use** `Io` for:\n//! - YAML syntax errors → use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n//! - Type mismatches → use [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`]\n//! - Constraint violations → use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`]\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use Io for file system errors\n//! let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;  // io::Error → Io\n//!\n//! // ❌ Incorrect: Don't use Io for parsing errors\n//! // let error = ParseError::io(\"invalid YAML structure\");  // Wrong! Use Syntax instead\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ### `ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8` vs Other Variants\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`] specifically for **encoding errors**:\n//! - Invalid UTF-8 byte sequences in input\n//! - Failed UTF-8 validation when converting bytes to strings\n//!\n//! **Do not use** `InvalidUtf8` for:\n//! - I/O errors during file reading → use [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n//! - General parsing failures → use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use InvalidUtf8 for encoding errors\n//! let string = String::from_utf8(bytes)?;  // FromUtf8Error → InvalidUtf8\n//!\n//! // ❌ Incorrect: Don't use InvalidUtf8 for file reading errors\n//! // let error = ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8);  // Wrong! Use Io instead\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ### `ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof` vs `ParseErrorKind::Syntax`\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof`] when **input ends prematurely**:\n//! - Incomplete YAML documents (missing closing brackets, braces, quotes)\n//! - Truncated files or streams\n//! - Multi-document YAML streams that end mid-document\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`] for **general YAML syntax violations**:\n//! - Invalid indentation\n//! - Invalid escape sequences\n//! - Malformed scalars or mappings\n//! - Any YAML grammar violation that's not specifically EOF-related\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use UnexpectedEof for incomplete input\n//! if input.ends_with(\"key: \") {\n//!     return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof));\n//! }\n//!\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use Syntax for general YAML errors\n//! if !is_valid_indentation(line) {\n//!     return Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid indentation\"));\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ### `ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch` vs `ParseErrorKind::Validation`\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`] when **a value has the wrong Rust/YAML type**:\n//! - Expecting an integer but finding a string\n//! - Expecting a sequence but finding a scalar\n//! - Expecting a boolean but finding a number\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`] for **semantic constraint violations**:\n//! - Value out of allowed range (e.g., port number > 65535)\n//! - String doesn't match required pattern (e.g., invalid email format)\n//! - Array length constraints violated\n//! - Business logic or schema validation failures\n//!\n//! The key distinction: `TypeMismatch` is about **type**, `Validation` is about **value**.\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use TypeMismatch for type errors\n//! let port = value[\"port\"].as_i64()\n//!     .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\"))?;\n//!\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use Validation for value constraints\n//! if port < 1 || port > 65535 {\n//!     return Err(ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\"));\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ### `ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey` vs `ParseErrorKind::Validation`\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey`] specifically for **duplicate key errors**:\n//! - YAML mappings with repeated keys (YAML 1.2 spec violation)\n//! - Configuration files with conflicting entries\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`] for **general validation failures** including:\n//! - Missing required fields (not duplicated, but absent)\n//! - Inter-field constraint violations (e.g., \"start_time > end_time\")\n//! - Schema validation failures beyond type/duplicate checking\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use DuplicateKey for duplicate keys\n//! if keys.contains(&key) {\n//!     return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey(key)));\n//! }\n//!\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use Validation for missing required fields\n//! if value[\"name\"].is_null() {\n//!     return Err(ParseError::validation(\"missing required field: 'name'\"));\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ### `ParseErrorKind::Other` (Catch-all) vs Specific Variants\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Other`] **only as a last resort** for errors that don't fit other categories:\n//! - Unclassifiable serde_yaml errors\n//! - Errors from external libraries that don't map cleanly\n//! - Temporary error cases during refactoring\n//!\n//! **Prefer specific variants** whenever possible:\n//! - Unknown anchors → [`ParseErrorKind::UnknownAnchor`]\n//! - Syntax errors → [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n//! - I/O errors → [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n//! - Type errors → [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`]\n//!\n//! If you find yourself using `Other` frequently, consider adding a new specific variant.\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! // ✅ Prefer specific variants\n//! return Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\"));\n//!\n//! // ⚠️ Use Other only as a catch-all\n//! return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::Other(\"unclassified error\".to_string())));\n//! ```\n//!\n//! # Error Propagation Strategy\n//!\n//! The `ParseError` type is designed to work seamlessly with Rust's error propagation\n//! mechanisms. It implements `From` for common error types, enabling the use of the `?`\n//! operator for automatic error conversion.\n//!\n//! ## Basic Propagation with `?`\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, Result};\n//!\n//! fn parse_config(path: &Path) -> Result<Config> {\n//!     let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;  // io::Error → ParseError\n//!     parse_yaml(&content)\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Adding Context with `.context()`\n//!\n//! Use the builder-style `.context()` method to add contextual information:\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! fn parse_database_config(value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<DatabaseConfig> {\n//!     let port = value[\"port\"]\n//!         .as_i64()\n//!         .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"null\"))\n//!         .context(\"while parsing database configuration\")?;\n//!\n//!     Ok(DatabaseConfig { port })\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Converting from Other Error Types\n//!\n//! `ParseError` implements `From` for:\n//! - `std::io::Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n//! - `serde_yaml::Error` → Appropriate [`ParseErrorKind`] based on error classification\n//! - `std::str::Utf8Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`]\n//! - `std::string::FromUtf8Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`]\n//!\n//! ## Custom Error Creation\n//!\n//! For domain-specific errors, create them directly:\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! fn validate_port(port: i64) -> Result<u16> {\n//!     if port < 1 || port > 65535 {\n//!         return Err(ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\"));\n//!     }\n//!     Ok(port as u16)\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! # Examples\n//!\n//! ## Basic Error Creation\n//!\n//! Creating errors using convenience constructors:\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n//!\n//! // Create a syntax error\n//! let syntax_err = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML indentation\");\n//! assert!(syntax_err.is_syntax());\n//!\n//! // Create a validation error\n//! let validation_err = ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\");\n//! assert!(validation_err.is_validation());\n//!\n//! // Create a type mismatch error\n//! let type_err = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\");\n//! assert!(type_err.is_type_mismatch());\n//! assert_eq!(type_err.summary(), \"<unknown>: type mismatch at 'port': expected integer, got string\");\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Error Propagation with `?`\n//!\n//! Using the `?` operator to automatically convert errors:\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, Result};\n//! use std::fs;\n//!\n//! fn read_config(path: &str) -> Result<String> {\n//!     // io::Error is automatically converted to ParseError via From impl\n//!     let content = fs::read_to_string(path)?;\n//!     Ok(content)\n//! }\n//!\n//! // This function demonstrates automatic error conversion\n//! fn parse_config_size(path: &str) -> Result<usize> {\n//!     let content = read_config(path)?;  // ParseError propagates via ?\n//!     Ok(content.len())\n//! }\n//!\n//! // Example: successful read\n//! assert!(parse_config_size(\"/dev/null\").is_ok());\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Custom Error Handling with Builder Pattern\n//!\n//! Using builder methods to add context and location information:\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n//!\n//! fn validate_service_config(yaml_content: &str) -> Result<(), ParseError> {\n//!     // Simulating an error during validation\n//!     if yaml_content.contains(\"port: abc\") {\n//!         let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"service.port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n//!             .with_path(\"config/services.yaml\")\n//!             .with_line(5)\n//!             .with_column(10)\n//!             .with_context(\"while validating service configuration\")\n//!             .with_snippet(\"services:\\n  - name: web\\n    port: abc\");\n//!\n//!         return Err(error);\n//!     }\n//!     Ok(())\n//! }\n//!\n//! // Create an error to verify the builder pattern\n//! let error = ParseError::validation(\"invalid port\")\n//!     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n//!     .with_line(10)\n//!     .with_column(5)\n//!     .with_context(\"while parsing configuration\");\n//!\n//! assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n//! assert_eq!(error.line, Some(10));\n//! assert_eq!(error.column, Some(5));\n//! assert_eq!(error.context, \"while parsing configuration\");\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Error Display and Formatting\n//!\n//! Different ways to format and display errors:\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n//!\n//! // Create an error with full context\n//! let error = ParseError::syntax(\"unexpected colon\")\n//!     .with_path(\"config/database.yaml\")\n//!     .with_line(15)\n//!     .with_column(8)\n//!     .with_context(\"while parsing database configuration\")\n//!     .with_snippet(\"database:\\n  host: localhost\\n  port: 5432:\\n    invalid\");\n//!\n//! // Get a single-line summary (good for logging)\n//! let summary = error.summary();\n//! assert!(summary.contains(\"config/database.yaml:15\"));\n//! assert!(summary.contains(\"syntax error\"));\n//! assert!(summary.contains(\"while parsing database configuration\"));\n//!\n//! // Get a detailed multi-line report (good for user display)\n//! let detailed = error.detailed_report();\n//! assert!(detailed.contains(\"error:\"));\n//! assert!(detailed.contains(\"snippet:\"));\n//!\n//! // Get a structured representation (good for debugging)\n//! let structured = error.format_structured();\n//! assert!(structured.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n//! assert!(structured.contains(\"config/database.yaml:15:8\"));\n//! assert!(structured.contains(\"line: Some(15)\"));\n//!\n//! // Display implementation provides user-friendly output\n//! let display_string = format!(\"{}\", error);\n//! assert!(display_string.contains(\"config/database.yaml\"));\n//! assert!(display_string.contains(\"syntax error\"));\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Error Conversion from Standard Types\n//!\n//! Automatic conversion from standard library errors:\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n//! use std::io;\n//!\n//! // io::Error converts to ParseError::Io\n//! let io_err = io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::NotFound, \"file not found\");\n//! let parse_err: ParseError = io_err.into();\n//! assert!(parse_err.is_io());\n//! assert!(parse_err.summary().contains(\"I/O error\"));\n//! assert!(parse_err.summary().contains(\"file not found\"));\n//!\n//! // String::from_utf8 Error converts to ParseError::InvalidUtf8\n//! let invalid_bytes = b\"\\xff\\xfe\\xfd\";\n//! let utf8_err = String::from_utf8(invalid_bytes.to_vec()).unwrap_err();\n//! let parse_err: ParseError = utf8_err.into();\n//! assert!(matches!(parse_err.kind, armor::parsers::yaml::ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8));\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Working with Error Types\n//!\n//! Checking error types and handling specific cases:\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n//!\n//! let errors = vec![\n//!     ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\"),\n//!     ParseError::io(\"file not found\"),\n//!     ParseError::validation(\"port out of range\"),\n//!     ParseError::type_mismatch(\"field\", \"string\", \"integer\"),\n//! ];\n//!\n//! // Check specific error types\n//! assert!(errors[0].is_syntax());\n//! assert!(errors[1].is_io());\n//! assert!(errors[2].is_validation());\n//! assert!(errors[3].is_type_mismatch());\n//!\n//! // Filter errors by type\n//! let syntax_errors: Vec<_> = errors.iter()\n//!     .filter(|e| e.is_syntax())\n//!     .collect();\n//! assert_eq!(syntax_errors.len(), 1);\n//! ```\n\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Main error type for YAML parsing operations\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct ParseError {\n    /// The kind of error that occurred\n    pub kind: ParseErrorKind,\n    /// The line number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n    pub line: Option<usize>,\n    /// The column number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n    pub column: Option<usize>,\n    /// The file or source path where the error occurred\n    pub path: Option<String>,\n    /// A code snippet showing the problematic segment\n    pub snippet: Option<String>,\n    /// Additional context about the error\n    pub context: String,\n}\n\nimpl ParseError {\n    /// Create a new ParseError with the given kind\n    ///\n    /// This is the base constructor for creating a ParseError. All fields except\n    /// `kind` are set to their default values (None or empty). Use the builder methods\n    /// to add additional context and location information.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, ParseErrorKind};\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof);\n    /// assert!(matches!(error.kind, ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof));\n    /// assert_eq!(error.line, None);\n    /// ```\n    ///\n    /// For common error types, prefer using the convenience constructors:\n    /// - [`ParseError::syntax()`] for syntax errors\n    /// - [`ParseError::io()`] for I/O errors\n    /// - [`ParseError::validation()`] for validation errors\n    /// - [`ParseError::type_mismatch()`] for type mismatch errors\n    pub fn new(kind: ParseErrorKind) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            kind,\n            line: None,\n            column: None,\n            path: None,\n            snippet: None,\n            context: String::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the line number for this error\n    ///\n    /// Line numbers are 1-indexed, matching typical text editor conventions.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n    ///     .with_line(42);\n    /// assert_eq!(error.line, Some(42));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn with_line(mut self, line: usize) -> Self {\n        self.line = Some(line);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the column number for this error\n    ///\n    /// Column numbers are 1-indexed, indicating the character position within the line.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n    ///     .with_column(15);\n    /// assert_eq!(error.column, Some(15));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn with_column(mut self, column: usize) -> Self {\n        self.column = Some(column);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the file/source path for this error\n    ///\n    /// The path can be absolute or relative. It's displayed in error messages to help\n    /// users locate the source of the error.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config/services.yaml\");\n    /// assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config/services.yaml\".to_string()));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn with_path(mut self, path: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.path = Some(path.into());\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the code snippet for this error\n    ///\n    /// The snippet should contain the relevant lines of source code where the error occurred.\n    /// This is displayed in detailed error reports to help users understand the context.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid port value\")\n    ///     .with_line(5)\n    ///     .with_snippet(\"service:\\n  name: web\\n  port: abc\");\n    ///\n    /// let report = error.detailed_report();\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"service:\"));\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"port: abc\"));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn with_snippet(mut self, snippet: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.snippet = Some(snippet.into());\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the context message for this error\n    ///\n    /// Context provides additional information about what operation was being performed\n    /// when the error occurred. This helps users understand the broader scenario.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::validation(\"port out of range\")\n    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing database configuration\");\n    ///\n    /// let summary = error.summary();\n    /// assert!(summary.contains(\"while parsing database configuration\"));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn with_context(mut self, context: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.context = context.into();\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set both line and column for this error\n    ///\n    /// Convenience method for setting location with a single call.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n    ///     .with_location(10, 25);\n    /// assert_eq!(error.line, Some(10));\n    /// assert_eq!(error.column, Some(25));\n    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"10:25\");\n    /// ```\n    pub fn with_location(mut self, line: usize, column: usize) -> Self {\n        self.line = Some(line);\n        self.column = Some(column);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Get a formatted location string (e.g., \"file.yaml:10:5\" or \"<unknown>:10\")\n    ///\n    /// This method generates a human-readable location string based on the available\n    /// location information (path, line, column). The format adapts based on which fields are set.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// // Full location with path, line, and column\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n    ///     .with_location(10, 5);\n    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"config.yaml:10:5\");\n    ///\n    /// // Line and column only\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n    ///     .with_location(42, 15);\n    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"42:15\");\n    ///\n    /// // Path only\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\");\n    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"config.yaml\");\n    ///\n    /// // Unknown location\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\");\n    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"<unknown>\");\n    /// ```\n    pub fn location_string(&self) -> String {\n        match (&self.path, self.line, self.column) {\n            (Some(path), Some(line), Some(col)) => format!(\"{}:{}:{}\", path, line, col),\n            (Some(path), Some(line), None) => format!(\"{}:{}\", path, line),\n            (Some(path), None, Some(col)) => format!(\"{}::{}\", path, col),\n            (Some(path), None, None) => path.clone(),\n            (None, Some(line), Some(col)) => format!(\"{}:{}\", line, col),\n            (None, Some(line), None) => format!(\"{}\", line),\n            (None, None, Some(col)) => format!(\"col {}\", col),\n            (None, None, None) => \"<unknown>\".to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a brief summary of the error (single line, suitable for logging)\n    ///\n    /// The summary includes location, error kind, and context (if set). This is ideal\n    /// for logging purposes where a compact, single-line error message is desired.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// // Summary with context\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n    ///     .with_line(10)\n    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing services\");\n    /// assert_eq!(error.summary(),\n    ///     \"config.yaml:10: syntax error: invalid token - while parsing services\");\n    ///\n    /// // Summary without context\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n    ///     .with_line(10);\n    /// assert_eq!(error.summary(),\n    ///     \"config.yaml:10: syntax error: invalid token\");\n    /// ```\n    pub fn summary(&self) -> String {\n        let location = self.location_string();\n        if !self.context.is_empty() {\n            format!(\"{}: {} - {}\", location, self.kind, self.context)\n        } else {\n            format!(\"{}: {}\", location, self.kind)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a detailed multi-line error report with snippet and visual indicator\n    ///\n    /// This method generates a comprehensive error report suitable for displaying\n    /// to users. It includes the error summary, context (if set), and a code snippet\n    /// with a visual indicator (^) pointing to the error location.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid port value\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n    ///     .with_line(5)\n    ///     .with_column(10)\n    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing service configuration\")\n    ///     .with_snippet(\"service:\\n  port: abc\");\n    ///\n    /// let report = error.detailed_report();\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"error:\"));\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"config.yaml:5:10\"));\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"syntax error: invalid port value\"));\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"while parsing service configuration\"));\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"snippet:\"));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn detailed_report(&self) -> String {\n        let mut report = String::new();\n\n        // Header line with location and error kind\n        report.push_str(&format!(\"error: {}\\n\", self.summary()));\n\n        // Add context section if present\n        if !self.context.is_empty() {\n            report.push_str(&format!(\"  context: {}\\n\", self.context));\n        }\n\n        // Add snippet with visual indicator\n        if let Some(snippet) = &self.snippet {\n            report.push_str(\"\\n  snippet:\\n\");\n            for line in snippet.lines() {\n                report.push_str(&format!(\"    {}\\n\", line));\n            }\n\n            // Add visual indicator for column position\n            if let Some(col) = self.column {\n                if col > 0 {\n                    report.push_str(&format!(\"    {}^\\n\", \" \".repeat(col.saturating_sub(1))));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        report\n    }\n\n    /// Format this error as a structured log entry (JSON-like but readable)\n    ///\n    /// This method produces a structured representation of the error suitable for\n    /// programmatic analysis or structured logging systems.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n    ///     .with_line(8)\n    ///     .with_column(10);\n    ///\n    /// let formatted = error.format_structured();\n    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"config.yaml:8:10\"));\n    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"line: Some(8)\"));\n    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"column: Some(10)\"));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn format_structured(&self) -> String {\n        format!(\n            \"ParseError {{ kind: {:?}, location: {}, line: {:?}, column: {:?} }}\",\n            self.kind,\n            self.location_string(),\n            self.line,\n            self.column\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Create a syntax error\n    pub fn syntax(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Syntax(msg.into()))\n    }\n\n    /// Create an I/O error\n    pub fn io(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Io(msg.into()))\n    }\n\n    /// Create a validation error\n    pub fn validation(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Validation(msg.into()))\n    }\n\n    /// Create a type mismatch error\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `field` - The field path where the error occurred\n    /// * `expected` - The expected type description\n    /// * `actual` - The actual type that was received\n    pub fn type_mismatch(field: impl Into<String>, expected: impl Into<String>, actual: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch {\n            field: field.into(),\n            expected: expected.into(),\n            actual: actual.into(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a syntax error\n    pub fn is_syntax(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::Syntax(_))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is an I/O error\n    pub fn is_io(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::Io(_))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a validation error\n    pub fn is_validation(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::Validation(_))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a type mismatch error\n    pub fn is_type_mismatch(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch { .. })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ParseError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        // Use the summary method for concise display\n        write!(f, \"{}\", self.summary())?;\n\n        // Add detailed section with snippet if available\n        if let Some(snippet) = &self.snippet {\n            write!(f, \"\\n\\n  snippet:\")?;\n            for line in snippet.lines() {\n                write!(f, \"\\n    {}\", line)?;\n            }\n\n            // Add visual indicator for column position\n            if let Some(col) = self.column {\n                if col > 0 {\n                    write!(f, \"\\n    {}\", \" \".repeat(col.saturating_sub(1)))?;\n                    write!(f, \"^\")?;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for ParseError {}\n\nimpl PartialEq for ParseError {\n    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n        // Only compare core error identification fields\n        // Context and snippet are NOT included in equality\n        self.kind == other.kind\n            && self.line == other.line\n            && self.column == other.column\n            && self.path == other.path\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Debug for ParseError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"ParseError\")\n            .field(\"kind\", &self.kind)\n            .field(\"location\", &self.location_string())\n            .field(\"line\", &self.line)\n            .field(\"column\", &self.column)\n            .field(\"path\", &self.path)\n            .field(\"context\", &self.context)\n            .field(\"has_snippet\", &self.snippet.is_some())\n            .finish()\n    }\n}\n\n/// The kind of parse error that occurred\n///\n/// This enum represents the core categories of errors that can occur during\n/// YAML parsing operations. Each variant represents a distinct class of error.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\npub enum ParseErrorKind {\n    /// Syntax error in the YAML source\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors related to invalid YAML structure, malformed\n    /// syntax, or violations of the YAML grammar rules.\n    Syntax(String),\n\n    /// I/O error (file not found, permission denied, etc.)\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors related to file system operations such as\n    /// reading, writing, or accessing files.\n    Io(String),\n\n    /// Validation error (constraint violations)\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors related to constraint violations such as\n    /// required field violations, range violations, or other schema validation failures.\n    Validation(String),\n\n    /// Type mismatch error (unexpected type for a field)\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors where a value has an unexpected type, such as\n    /// expecting a string but receiving a number, or expecting a sequence but receiving a scalar.\n    TypeMismatch {\n        /// The field path where the error occurred\n        field: String,\n        /// The expected type\n        expected: String,\n        /// The actual type that was received\n        actual: String,\n    },\n\n    /// Unexpected end of input\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors where the YAML source ends prematurely\n    /// and cannot be fully parsed.\n    UnexpectedEof,\n\n    /// Invalid UTF-8 encoding\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors where the input contains invalid UTF-8 sequences.\n    InvalidUtf8,\n\n    /// Unknown anchor or alias\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors where an anchor or alias reference cannot be resolved.\n    UnknownAnchor(String),\n\n    /// Duplicate key in mapping\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors where duplicate keys are found in a YAML mapping.\n    DuplicateKey(String),\n\n    /// Other error\n    ///\n    /// This variant provides a catch-all for errors that don't fit into any of the\n    /// other specific categories, allowing for extensibility.\n    Other(String),\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ParseErrorKind {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Syntax(msg) => write!(f, \"syntax error: {}\", msg),\n            Self::Io(msg) => write!(f, \"I/O error: {}\", msg),\n            Self::Validation(msg) => write!(f, \"validation error: {}\", msg),\n            Self::TypeMismatch { field, expected, actual } => {\n                write!(f, \"type mismatch at '{}': expected {}, got {}\", field, expected, actual)\n            }\n            Self::UnexpectedEof => write!(f, \"unexpected end of input\"),\n            Self::InvalidUtf8 => write!(f, \"invalid UTF-8 encoding\"),\n            Self::UnknownAnchor(name) => write!(f, \"unknown anchor: {}\", name),\n            Self::DuplicateKey(key) => write!(f, \"duplicate key: {}\", key),\n            Self::Other(msg) => write!(f, \"error: {}\", msg),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result type alias for parse operations\npub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, ParseError>;\n\n// ============================================================================\n// From implementations for error propagation\n// ============================================================================\n\nimpl From<std::io::Error> for ParseError {\n    fn from(err: std::io::Error) -> Self {\n        ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::Io(err.to_string()))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<serde_yaml::Error> for ParseError {\n    fn from(err: serde_yaml::Error) -> Self {\n        // Classify serde_yaml errors Integration tests for ParseError error propagation\n//!\n//! This test suite demonstrates error propagation through a call stack using\n//! the Result<T, ParseError> pattern and the ? operator.\n\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, Result};\nuse std::io;\nuse std::path::Path;\n\n/// Simulates reading a configuration file\nfn read_config_file(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {\n    // This will fail since the file doesn't exist\n    // The io::Error automatically converts to ParseError via the From impl\n    let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;\n    Ok(content)\n}\n\n/// Simulates parsing YAML content\nfn parse_yaml_content(content: &str) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n    // This will fail if the YAML is invalid\n    // serde_yaml::Error automatically converts to ParseError via the From impl\n    let value = serde_yaml::from_str(content)?;\n    Ok(value)\n}\n\n/// Simulates validating a specific field from the parsed YAML\nfn extract_database_config(value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<DatabaseConfig> {\n    // Extract database configuration with context\n    let db_section = value.get(\"database\")\n        .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::validation(\"missing 'database' section\")\n            .with_context(\"while extracting database configuration\"))?;\n\n    let host = db_section.get(\"host\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n        .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"database.host\", \"string\", \"null\")\n            .with_context(\"while extracting database host\"))?;\n\n    let port = db_section.get(\"port\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_i64())\n        .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"database.port\", \"integer\", \"null\")\n            .with_context(\"while extracting database port\"))?;\n\n    Ok(DatabaseConfig {\n        host: host.to_string(),\n        port: port as u16,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Simulates a complete configuration loading workflow\nfn load_config_from_file(path: &Path) -> Result<DatabaseConfig> {\n    // This demonstrates error propagation through a call stack:\n    // 1. read_config_file can fail with io::Error → ParseError\n    // 2. parse_yaml_content can fail with serde_yaml::Error → ParseError\n    // 3. extract_database_config can fail with custom ParseError\n\n    let content = read_config_file(path)?;\n    let yaml = parse_yaml_content(&content)?;\n    let config = valid: yaml: [\";\n    let serde_err = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(invalid_yaml).unwrap_err();\n    let parse_err: ParseError = serde_err.into();\n\n    // Should classify as an error (syntax or other depending on the error message)\n    // Just verify it converted successfully and contains error info\n    let err_str = parse_err.to_string();\n    assert!(!err_str.is_empty(), \"Error message should not be empty\");\n\n    // Verify it's one of the expected error kinds\n    assert!(parse_err.is_syntax() || parse_err.to_string().contains(\"error\"),\n            \"Error            })\n79\t    }\n80\t\n81\t    // Test type mismatch error creation\n82\t    let invalid_value = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(\"port: \\\"8080\\\"\").unwrap();\n83\t    let test_error_creation_from_nested_parsing_context() {\n95\t    /// Simulates nested structure parsing with error accumulation\n96\t    fn extract_nested_field(value: &serde_yaml::Value, path: &str) -> Result<String> {\n97\t        let parts: Vec<&str> = path.split('.').collect();\n98\t        let mut current = value;\n99\t\n100\t        for (i, part) in parts.iter().enumerate() {\n101\t            match current.get(part) {\n102\t                Some(next) => current = next,\n103\t                None => {\n104\t                    return Err(ParseError::validation(\n105\t                        format!(\"missing field: {}\", part)\n106\t                    )\n107\t                    .with_context(format!(\n108\t                        \"while navigating nested structure at level {} (path: {})\",\n109\t                        i,\n110\t                        path\n111\t                    )))\n112\t                }\n113\t            }\n114\t        }\n115\t\n116\t        current.as_str()\n117\t            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n118\t            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\n119\t                path,\n120\t                \"string\",\n121\t                \"null or other type\"\n122\t            ).with_context(format!(\"while extracting final value from path: {}\", path)))\n123\t    }\n124\t\n125\t    let yaml = r#\"database:\n126\t  connection:\n127\t    timeout: 30\"#;\n128\t    let value = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(yaml).unwrap();\n129\t\n130\t    // Test successful extraction\n131\t    let result = extract_nested_field(&value, \"database.connection.timeout\");\n132\t    assert!(result.is_err()); // timeout is a number, not string\n133\t\n134\t    // Test missing field error\n135\t    let result = extract_nested_field(&value, \"database.connection.missing\");\n136\t    assert!(result.is_err());\n137\t    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n138\t    assert!(error.context.contains(\"while navigating nested structure\"));\n139\t    assert!(error.context.contains(\"level\"));\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t// ===========================================================================\n143\t// ===========================================================================\n272\t// Error Propagation Through Result Types\n273\t// ===========================================================================\n274\t\n275\t#[test]\n276\tfn test_error_propagation_through_parsing_pipeline() {\n277\t    fn read_file(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {\n278\t        std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n279\t            .map_err(|e| ParseError::from(e).with_context(\"while reading file\"))\n280\t    }\n281\t\n282\t    fn }\n286\t\n287\t    fn validate_config(value: serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n288\t        // Simulate validation\n289\t        if value.get(\"required_field\").is_none() {\n290\t            return Err(ParseError::validation(\"missing required field: required_field\")\n291\t                .with_context(\"while validating configuration structure\"));\n292\t        }\n293\t        Ok(value)\n294\t    }\n295\t\n296\t    fn full_pipeline(path: &Path) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n297\t        let content = read_file(path)?;\n298\t        let yaml = parse_yaml(&content)?;\n299\t        let validated = validate_config(yaml)?;\n300\t        Ok(validated)\n301\t    }\n302\t\n303\t    // Test error propagation at file reading stage\n304\t    let result = file\"));\n308\t}\n309\t\n310\t#[test]\n311\tfn test_error_propagation_with_context_accumulation() {\n312\t    fn level_3_operation() -> Result<String> {\n313\t        Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n314\t            .with_line(10)\n315\t            .with_column(5)\n316\t            .with_context(\"while parsing token\"))\n317\t    }\n318\t\n319\t    fn level_2_operation() -> Result<String> {\n320\t        level_3_operation()\n321\t            .map_err(|e| e.with_context(\"while processing statement\"))\n322\t    }\n323\t\n324\t    fn level_1_operation() -> Result<String> {\n325\t        level_2_operation()\n326\t            .map_err(|e| e.with_context(\"while parsing function body\"))\n327\t    }\n328\t\n329\t    let result = level_1_operation();\n330\t    assert!(result.is_err());\n331\t\n332\t    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n333\t    // Context should be accumulated through the chain\n334\t    assert!(error.context.contains(\"while parsing
